reasoning: The document maintains high content fidelity (0.988) and excellent programmatic recall (0.974), but the structural score (0.772) is significantly lower due to recurring table corruption. All three table sections contain header rows that fail to span the full grid width (colspan: 1 instead of 2), creating an inconsistent structural grid that may impact accessibility. Given the 3x cost penalty for bad document auto-approval, this pervasive structural issue warrants human judgment.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: There is a minor wording discrepancy in section 1.3. The output inserts the word 'and' and pluralizes 'building', likely due to OCR errors. The source text reads 'streams, administrative boundaries, elevation, building footprints' while the output reads 'streams, and administrative boundaries, elevation, buildings footprints'.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: SMAC- Prioritize streams, administrative boundaries, elevation, building footprints
concerns.output_quote: SMAC- Prioritize streams, and administrative boundaries, elevation, buildings footprints

concerns.analysis: The table structure in all three sections is corrupted. The objective headers (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1, etc.) occupy a single row spanning the full width of the table in the source. In the JSON output, these rows contain only a single cell with 'colspan: 1', despite the table having two columns in the rows immediately below them. This creates a structural mismatch where the row/column grid is inconsistent.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document shows objective headers (e.g., '1.1. Promote free and open discovery...') as full-width rows spanning both columns of the table below it.
concerns.location: Sections 1, 2, and 3; Tables at reading_order 6, 8, and 10

action_items: Repair the HTML table structure to ensure header rows correctly span all columns (colspan: 2).
Correct minor text errors in section 1.3 to match the source PDF exactly.
